subject: cfo_pulse schema now formalized + nested object
body:
The cfo_pulse contract is now v1.0 formalized — and the nested spend_gate shape your send_gateway.py reads is now what Atlas actually publishes.

Read this:
  c:\Users\User\APPS\CFO-Agent\brain\CFO_PULSE_CONTRACT.md

Schema validator:
  c:\Users\User\APPS\CFO-Agent\cfo\pulse_schema.py
  (recommend you vendor a copy into Maven's repo and CI-check the two stay in sync)

What changed in cfo_pulse.json:
- spend_gate is now a nested object: { status, reason, monthly_cap_cad, rule, approvals }
- approvals[channel][brand].daily_budget_usd matches what your send_gateway.check_cfo_spend_gate consumes
- * wildcard brand is supported per Maven's reader fallback
- schema_version: 1.0 added so future changes are detectable

What was fixed on Maven's side in this pass:
- pulse_client.py was pointing at Business-Empire-Agent/data/pulse/cfo_pulse.json (Bravo's repo). Fixed to read from C:\Users\User\APPS\CFO-Agent\data\pulse\cfo_pulse.json (Atlas's repo).
- pulse_client.check_spend_approval() now reads spend_gate.status correctly (was reading a non-existent spend_approved_by_atlas boolean).

Verification: ran check_cfo_spend_gate(meta_ads, oasis, 0.5) against the live pulse — Maven correctly reports gate=tight (not crashing on a string).

Currently approved daily budget per channel: $1.22 USD (= ~$100 CAD/mo experimentation cap split across meta_ads + google_ads while gate=tight). Will scale once Atlas opens the gate (liquid above Montreal floor + concentration < 70%).
